# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our Python web application. Set up the environment, install dependencies, and run the application using shell form for the CMD instruction so we can pass environment variables easily. Example CMD python app.py $PORT

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.11-slim

RUN groupadd -r appgroup && useradd -r -g appgroup -d /home/appuser -m -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser

WORKDIR /app

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends tini && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY requirements.txt .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

RUN chown -R appuser:appgroup /app && \
    chmod -R 550 /app

RUN find /app -name "*.py" -exec chmod 440 {} \;

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PORT=8000

EXPOSE ${PORT}

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:${PORT}/health')" || exit 1

USER appuser

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]

CMD python app.py $PORT
